content: Human language learning's formative reflection (from the high school English Unit 9 vocabulary context of Reflexion) offers a blueprint to fix a key flaw in current LLM self-reflection: unlike LLM reflection that mostly only revises incorrect outputs, human academic reflection anchors improvement to explicit, repeated practice of core language and reasoning vocabulary, which can be adapted to train LLMs to build a 'core reasoning vocabulary' of error patterns. This structured, vocabulary-like iterative reflection would make LLM self-reflection more systematic rather than ad-hoc.
